perforce.submenu.refresh.label: Refresh
perforce.config.enabled.description: Enable the Perforce source control integration.
perforce.config.port.description: Fallback P4PORT (server:port) used when no environment variable or P4CONFIG provides one. Prefer p4 set / P4CONFIG.
perforce.config.user.description: Fallback P4USER used when no environment variable or P4CONFIG provides one.
perforce.config.client.description: Fallback P4CLIENT (workspace) used when no environment variable or P4CONFIG provides one.
perforce.config.maxConcurrent.descriptio: Maximum number of concurrent p4 processes. Each p4 command is a process spawn plus a server round-trip, so a low cap protects the server and this machine.
perforce.config.refreshInterval.descript: Seconds between background refreshes of the pending changelists. 0 disables polling (recommended for large depots); use explicit Refresh instead.
perforce.config.autoEdit.description: Automatically open a file for edit (p4 edit) on its first local modification. Off by default — opening files for edit is a server operation.
perforce.config.autoReconcile.descriptio: Automatically scan the working tree for uncollected changes (files edited, created, or deleted on disk but not opened) on every refresh, populating the "Changes to Reconcile" group. Off by default — the scan (p4 reconcile -n) can be slow on large workspaces; use Refresh (Clean) or Collect Changes to run it on demand.
perforce.config.autoRefresh.description: Watch the workspace on disk and refresh automatically when files change — a save in the editor, or an edit from an external tool. Populates the "Changes to Reconcile" group (via p4 reconcile -n) so drifted files surface without a manual Refresh (Clean). On by default and debounced; turn it off on very large depots where the reconcile scan is slow, and use Refresh (Clean) on demand instead.
perforce.config.cache.enabled.descriptio: Cache p4 and Swarm results to cut server round-trips. Immutable p4 data persists across sessions; mutable workspace and review data uses a short TTL and is invalidated after any change.
perforce.config.cache.workspaceTtl.descr: Milliseconds a cached workspace query (opened files, file mappings) stays valid before it is re-fetched. Cache is always invalidated after a mutation, so this only bounds staleness from changes made outside the editor. 0 disables workspace caching (immutable-history caching still applies).
perforce.config.cache.diskLimitMb.descri: Maximum size (MB) of the on-disk cache for immutable history data. Least-recently-used entries are evicted past this cap. 0 disables disk persistence (in-memory caching still applies).
perforce.command.swarm.ping.title: Swarm: Check Connection
perforce.config.swarm.enabled.descriptio: Enable the Swarm (P4 Code Review) integration.
perforce.config.swarm.url.description: Swarm server URL. Credentials are never stored here — Swarm reuses the p4 login ticket.
perforce.config.swarm.apiVersion.descrip: Swarm REST API version segment (default v9 — its endpoint set is the most complete, including the action dashboard; use v11 only if your server requires it).
perforce.config.swarm.pollInterval.descr: Seconds between background polls for review updates (drives new-review notifications, the Activity Bar badge and the status bar count). 0 uses the default 60s. Minimum 10s.
perforce.config.swarm.reviewWindowDays.d: Only list reviews updated within this many days in the Swarm Reviews view; ignored reviews older than this are automatically removed from the Ignored list. 0 shows all reviews with no time limit.
perforce.config.swarm.authMode.descripti: How Swarm authenticates: 'ticket' reuses the p4 login ticket (default), 'token' uses an independent Swarm API token from secret storage.
perforce.config.swarm.trace.description: Log verbose Swarm diagnostics (request bodies, query params, retries, timings, error response bodies) to the Swarm output channel. Credentials are never logged. Use when troubleshooting.
